title: AMETEK, Inc. (AME) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of AMETEK (AME) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 75/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 8.0% free cash flow growth rate reflects AME's proven ability to expand organically at a modest pace while continually layering on margin-accretive, bolt-on acquisitions. This dual-engine approach, combined with relentless operational efficiency, drives highly predictable compounding.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate is utilized. AME's massive diversification across hundreds of niche end markets (aerospace, medical, automation) significantly insulates it from isolated economic shocks, lowering its overall equity risk profile compared to a cyclical industrial.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal growth rate assumes AME will grow slightly faster than long-term GDP, propelled by structural mega-trends like global industrial automation, precision manufacturing, and the increasing complexity of medical devices.

thesisParagraphs: AMETEK represents a masterclass in industrial compounding. The company operates entirely outside the spotlight, manufacturing the highly specialized, boring, yet mission-critical instruments and electromechanical devices that power modern industry. By targeting fragmented niche markets, AME avoids direct competition with massive conglomerates and establishes deep, sticky relationships with its customers based on precision and reliability, not price.

This strategy creates an immensely powerful cash generation engine. AME relentlessly extracts operational efficiencies from its existing businesses to fund a continuous pipeline of highly accretive, bolt-on acquisitions. However, the market has fully recognized the brilliance of this model. Trading at a premium multiple, the margin of safety is currently negative. It is an exceptional company trading at a price that leaves little room for execution missteps or a broader industrial slowdown.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick an 8.0% growth rate for AME?
faq.answer: This rate explicitly models AME's historic 'Growth Playbook,' combining low single-digit organic growth driven by pricing power and innovation with a consistent cadence of highly accretive M&A funded by its massive free cash flow generation.

faq.question: Is AME vulnerable to a recession?
faq.answer: While not immune to a severe global industrial downturn, AME is far less cyclical than traditional manufacturers. Its extreme diversification across end markets (medical, aerospace, food/beverage) and its focus on critical, non-discretionary instruments buffer its earnings significantly.

faq.question: Why is the verdict OVERVALUED?
faq.answer: AMETEK is a phenomenal business, but its compounding strategy is widely recognized and priced into the stock. The current valuation demands near-flawless execution of its M&A strategy and assumes a benign macroeconomic environment, compressing the margin of safety.

prospectVerdictDetail: AMETEK operates as a premier industrial compounder, meticulously executing a dual strategy of operational excellence and relentless, bolt-on acquisitions. It manufactures highly engineered, mission-critical electronic instruments and electromechanical devices across diverse, niche markets (aerospace, medical, industrial automation). This specialization creates a wide moat characterized by high switching costs and significant pricing power. The sheer diversity of its end markets buffers it against isolated macroeconomic shocks, cementing its status as a robust compounder.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: AME achieves consistent, high-single to low-double-digit growth through a proven playbook: organic innovation in specialized instruments combined with a continuous pipeline of highly accretive acquisitions.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: While core organic growth is typically modest, AME consistently outperforms broader industrial peers by layering on highly complementary acquisitions. This dual engine drives predictable, low-volatility revenue expansion across economic cycles.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: AME intentionally targets fragmented, niche markets. It rapidly consolidates share by acquiring smaller competitors, integrating them into its global distribution network, and expanding their margins via its operational excellence initiatives.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Its pricing power is exceptional within its specific niches. AME produces highly specialized, often customized, components where performance and reliability far outweigh price sensitivity for its industrial and aerospace customers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Innovation focuses on incremental, customer-driven improvements in precision measurement and automation. While not exhibiting the rapid product cycles of consumer tech, AME invests heavily in R&D to maintain technical superiority in its specialized domains.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: AME's moat is remarkably durable, built on a foundation of high switching costs, deep technical expertise, and a sprawling, diversified portfolio of mission-critical industrial solutions.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs are immense. AME's instruments are deeply integrated into customers' manufacturing processes, testing protocols, or final aerospace/medical products. Rerouting complex supply chains to save marginally on specialized components is rarely viable.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Traditional network effects are minimal in specialized industrial manufacturing. However, AME leverages immense scale advantages across its global footprint, optimizing supply chains and cross-selling newly acquired technologies.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Its IP position is profound. AME holds extensive patents and proprietary knowledge across over 100 brands, creating a massive barrier to entry for any competitor attempting to replicate its breadth of highly specialized instruments.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: This factor evaluates to a perfect score. Despite being a manufacturer, AME generates massive free cash flow due to its high-margin, specialized products and relentless focus on operational efficiency and cost control, fueling its acquisition machine.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment for AME is highly positive, rewarding its boring but highly effective compounding strategy. The ongoing mega-trends of industrial automation and reshoring act as powerful long-term tailwinds.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward as AME integrates newly acquired companies and aggressively expands operating margins, demonstrating the immense scalability of its business model.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is anchored in reliability and execution. AME is widely viewed as a premier industrial compounder, insulated from singular cyclical shocks by its vast diversification across medical, aerospace, and general industrial sectors.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management executes a near-flawless, programmatic M&A strategy. They acquire specialized, high-margin businesses, relentlessly optimize their cost structures, and redeploy the generated free cash flow into the next acquisition, compounding shareholder value.

prospectKeyRisks: A severe, prolonged global industrial recession could negatively impact organic growth and delay capital expenditures across its diverse end markets.
The risk of a significant misstep in its programmatic M&A strategy, such as overpaying for an acquisition or failing to effectively integrate and extract operational synergies.
Supply chain disruptions or raw material cost inflation that could temporarily squeeze operating margins if it cannot rapidly pass costs through via pricing.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued M&A at attractive prices in fragmented instrumentation markets, with $1.5B+ in annual acquisition capacity funded from free cash flow and moderate leverage
Margin expansion toward 30%+ operating margins as recent acquisitions are integrated and the AMETEK Business System drives cost reductions across the portfolio
Secular growth in precision measurement demand driven by quality control automation, environmental monitoring regulations, and advanced manufacturing requirements for EV/semiconductor production
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored AME at 84/100 and Opus at 68/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
